settings.heading: Terms & Conditions
settings.description: The Colic Assurance Program™ is a reimbursement program operated by Assurance Health Program, LLC.

settings.content: The Colic Assurance Program (CAP) is a reimbursement program operated by Assurance Health Program, LLC that covers qualifying horses for colic-related veterinary expenses up to $15,000 lifetime maximum.

• Formally accepted into the program via enrollment form
  • Reside in the United States or Canada
  • Under care of a licensed veterinarian
  • Fully weaned and at least five months old
  • Cannot have had more than one abdominal surgery
  • Cannot be miniature horses, donkeys, burros, mules, zebras, or zorses

Ineligible conditions include: diagnosis of lipomas or enterolithiasis; chronic orthopedic conditions or metabolic disease; cases where the attending veterinarian cannot rule out non-colic causes.

Horses must purchase and administer qualifying products — Assure Guard, Assure Plus, or Assure Guard Gold — at minimum doses based on assigned wellness programs.

Purchase timeline:

  • No more than 48 hours before enrollment submission
  • No later than five business days after formal acceptance
  • Initial purchase through veterinarian, approved retailer, or Arenus

Maximum benefits vary by history:

  • No prior colic: $15,000 lifetime
  • Prior colic history: varies by colic episodes
  • One abdominal surgery history: $3,750 months 13–24; potentially $7,500 thereafter
  • Three or more colics during enrollment: program disqualification

Per-incident limits:

  • Medical colic: $800 maximum per episode
  • Surgical colic: one episode covered

• Annual physical examinations by licensed veterinarian
  • Annual dental examinations and treatments
  • Core vaccinations (tetanus, EEE/WEE, WNV, rabies)
  • Veterinarian-directed deworming program documentation
  • Purchase records for all qualifying products
  • Medical records and proof of payment for treated colic episodes
  • Complete 12-month veterinary health history via affidavit
Owners must report all colic episodes within 14 calendar days, regardless of claim filing. Failure to report renders horses ineligible for future benefits.
All required forms must be submitted within 30 calendar days of treatment completion, including the Primary Care Veterinarian Form, Attending Veterinarian Form, and Reimbursement Claim Form.
Assurance Health Program, LLC may cancel with 90 days' notice. Owners may discontinue anytime via the Discontinuation Form. False or fraudulent claims result in disqualification.

Compliance with the terms of this Agreement does not constitute a guarantee that a Qualifying Horse will not experience colic.

The program is not intended to be a substitute for insurance and maximum liability is limited to applicable reimbursement amounts.
